div1.entryFree: Ajax, the Greek hero, son of Telamon: Lucr. 1394. Lucr. 1394 LLL IV, 3, 7 (as mad as A.). Aeacides was A. Shr. III, 1, 53. like A. Telamonius, on sheep or oxen could I spend my fury, H6B V, 1, 26. the Greeks upon advice did bury A. that slew himself, Tit. I, 379. A. is their fool (i. e. a fool to them) Lr. II, 2, 132. the seven-fold shield of A. Ant. IV, 14, 38. Cymb. IV, 2, 252. Troil. I, 2, 14 (and passim). A quibble with a jakes: your lion, that holds his pollaxe sitting on a close-stool, will be given to A. LLL V, 2, 581; and perhaps Troil. II, 1, 70.
